nodes.label: AI as literature screening tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use the Vet Explainer add-on of chat GPT a lot to help me summarize the papers to make sure they're relevant without having to read the entire thing

nodes.label: AI as pitfall identifier in studies
nodes.label_negative: AI missing contextual and author biases
nodes.grounding_span: it does a really good job at identifying pitfalls of scientific studies

nodes.label: AI for evaluating research robustness and reproducibility
nodes.label_negative: AI inability to handle subjective definitions across disciplines
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to be able to have AI grade the robustness and reproducibility of research

nodes.label: Expert manual standardization vs AI delegation
nodes.label_negative: AI delegation of nuanced standardization tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would rather use my own expertise and cite studies to back up my standardization choices than ask AI to perform the task

nodes.label: Research trustworthiness and reproducibility
nodes.grounding_span: I'm really passionate about trying to improve the trustworthiness of scientific research

nodes.label: Efficient literature processing
nodes.grounding_span: help me summarize the papers to make sure they're relevant without having to read the entire thing

nodes.label: Expert autonomy and judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I would rather use my own expertise

nodes.label: Trust in AI for screening but not full reliance
nodes.grounding_span: I don't trust it fully - I use it as a quick summary to tell me if the paper is worth reading

nodes.label: Positive appreciation for AI pitfall detection
nodes.grounding_span: it does a really good job at identifying pitfalls of scientific studies - that's one of my favorite uses of AI

nodes.label: Aspirational desire for AI in robustness evaluation
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to be able to have AI grade the robustness and reproducibility of research

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI handling subjective definitions
nodes.grounding_span: I don't know if that's ever possible

nodes.label: Expert-driven manual verification — prefers own expertise for nuanced tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would rather use my own expertise and cite studies to back up my standardization choices

nodes.label: Contextual and institutional awareness — considers author bias and funding
nodes.grounding_span: identifying author bias requires looking at their publication history, their institutional affiliation
